title: #[allow(dead_code)] on set_subscription silences warning in wrong direction
description: store.rs set_subscription() is annotated #[allow(dead_code)] because Phase 3 hasn't connected the call site yet. This is backwards: dead_code warnings are valuable precisely as a signal that Phase 3 work needs to wire this up. Silencing the warning means the function can bitrot (signature changes, logic errors) without any compiler feedback until Phase 3 is attempted. Suggested rewrite: remove #[allow(dead_code)]; the warning is the correct behavior. When Phase 3 calls the function, the warning naturally disappears.
